features.description: Obsidian plugin scaffold using TypeScript builds successfully.
Plugin has a valid Obsidian manifest.json for local installation.
Registers command named Gentle Memories: Show memory.
Settings tab exposes journalTags, showOnStartup, minDaysBetweenStartupShows, aiEnabled, apiKey, and cacheAiResponses.
Discovers Markdown notes tagged with #journal, #diary, or #note by default.
Does not discover notes without configured tags.
Startup display respects showOnStartup.
Startup display respects minDaysBetweenStartupShows.
Manual command displays a memory even when startup display is disabled.
Shown memory contains title, excerpt, required buttons, and date when derivable.
Open note button opens the source note.
Next button does not repeat the same note when another eligible note exists.
Display history persists across plugin reloads.
AI is disabled by default.
No network request occurs before the user clicks Generate reflection.
No network request occurs while AI is disabled.
AI request payload excludes full note content, file paths, vault names, and display history.
AI cache is keyed by ${path}:${contentHash}.
Empty or unusable notes are not shown.
Manual verification plan scenarios from SPEC.md section 14 are documented and runnable.
Settings UI shows only the API key input for the selected AI provider while preserving hidden provider keys.
Debug mode adds a settings-tab show-memory control and privacy-safe diagnostic logging.
Memory display renders rich Obsidian Markdown note content with AI reflection first and Show more or Show less for long notes.
AI prompt asks providers to respond in the note excerpt language with a warm reading lead-in that may be summary, reflection, encouragement, or self-reflection.
Rename the AI reading prompt button from Generate reading prompt to Memories while preserving the same behavior.
Reduce the default long-note preview length so memories preserve more mystery before Show more.
Automatically generate or load the AI lead-in when AI is enabled, while visually separating it from the original note content and preserving privacy constraints.
Style the AI lead-in section with theme-aware visual separation from original note content.
